message.abstract:
<jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                <jats:p>We have performed detailed modeling of the COVID-19 epidemic within the State of Illinois at the population level, and within the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign at a more detailed level of description that follows individual students as they go about their educational and social activities.</jats:p>
                <jats:p>We ask the following questions:</jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:list list-type="order">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>How many COVID-19 cases are expected to be detected by entry screening?</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Will this initial “bump” in cases be containable using the mitigation steps being undertaken at UIUC?</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:p>
                <jats:p>Our answers are:</jats:p>
                <jats:p>
                  <jats:list list-type="order">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        Assuming that there are approximately 45,000 students returning to campus in the week beginning August 15, 2020, our most conservative estimate predicts that a median of 270 ± 90 (minimum-maximum range) COVID-19 positive cases will be detected by entry screening. The earliest estimate for entry screening that we report was made on July 24
                        <jats:sup>th</jats:sup>
                        and predicted 198 ± 90 (68% CI) positive cases.
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>If the number of returning students is less, then our estimate just needs to be scaled proportionately.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>This initial bump will be contained by entry screening initiated isolation and contact tracing, and once the semester is underway, by universal masking, a hybrid teaching model, twice-weekly testing, isolation, contact tracing, quarantining and the use of the Safer Illinois exposure notification app.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:p>
title: Entry screening and multi-layer mitigation of COVID-19 cases for a safe university reopening

reference.article-title: Modeling COVID-19 Spread in Small Colleges
